nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment configuration file documenting all runtime settings for the medical RAG system, including LLM provider selection (Ollama or OpenAI), model names, storage paths, and auto-ingestion flag.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports a dual-provider strategy: Ollama for fully local/private inference and OpenAI as a cloud fallback — the LLM_PROVIDER variable switches between them at runtime without code changes.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation covering the medical RAG system's purpose, hybrid retrieval architecture, privacy rationale for local LLM inference, supported file formats, setup instructions, and development roadmap across 8 sections.

nodes.name: SETUP_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive step-by-step setup guide covering Docker-based deployment, Ollama LLM installation, environment configuration, file ingestion methods, example queries, troubleshooting tips, and project structure overview across 19 sections.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration defining four services — PostgreSQL, ChromaDB, FastAPI backend, and Streamlit frontend — on a shared bridge network with persistent volumes and health checks for the medical RAG system.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a host.docker.internal gateway pattern so the containerised backend can reach Ollama running on the host machine without containerising the LLM itself, preserving GPU access and avoiding model duplication.

nodes.name: postgres
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 16 service providing structured relational storage for patient facts and structured records, exposed on port 5432 with a health check and persistent volume.

nodes.name: chromadb
nodes.summary: ChromaDB 0.5.3 vector database service providing semantic similarity search for the RAG pipeline, exposed on port 8001 with a persistent volume for indexed documents.

nodes.name: backend
nodes.summary: FastAPI backend service built from the ./backend context, exposing the RAG API on port 8080 and mounting the bucket and data directories for document ingestion and schema metadata.

nodes.name: frontend
nodes.summary: Streamlit frontend service built from the ./frontend context, serving the chat UI on port 8501 with live-reload volume mount and a backend URL environment variable.

nodes.name: todo.md
nodes.summary: Development task tracker documenting planned architectural improvements including schema-on-read with concept-map architecture, Text-to-SQL fallback, MIMIC-III dataset integration, and a completed-items reference section.
